pageTitle.value: Robert Lepage and Matías Umpierrez - Video

pageCaption.value: Canada’s Robert Lepage is world famous for his mastery of multidimensional productions. With his global outlook and mastery of technology, his protégé, Matías Umpierrez of Argentina, is also pushing the boundaries of theatre. Their pairing produced a fascinating exchange that Lepage described as “the best relationship possible”.

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégé in Theatre, 2016-2017

text.value: Canada’s Robert Lepage is world famous for his mastery of multidimensional productions. With his global outlook and mastery of technology, his protégé, Matías Umpierrez of Argentina, is also pushing the boundaries of theatre. Their pairing produced a fascinating exchange that Lepage described as “the best relationship possible”.

pageTitle.value: Jennifer Tipton and Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez - Video

pageCaption.value: Jennifer Tipton, one of the world’s greatest lighting designers, declares that “light is the substance of our existence”. Her protégé, Mexico’s Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez, agrees completely as he witnessed Tipton creating lighting for performances at theatres and opera houses all over the world.

WaPageTitle.value: Jennifer Tipton and Sebastián Solórzano Rodríguez - Video

pageTitle.value: Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib - Video

pageCaption.value: With a live performance and both Chicago and Beirut as the backdrop, Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib give surprising – and very different – explanations of the nature of art, as well as describing each other, their mentorship and how to change the world.

WaPageTitle.value: Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib - Video

metadescription.value: With a live performance and both Chicago and Beirut as the backdrop, Peter Sellars and Maya Zbib give surprising – and very different – explanations of the nature of art, as well as describing each other, their mentorship and how to change the world.

pageTitle.value: Julie Taymor and Selina Cartmell - Video

pageCaption.value: Julie Taymor, famous for creating spectacular theatre, film and opera, wanted to mentor “someone I can have a dialogue with”. She found her in British director Selina Cartmell, for whom the mentorship is an ideal opportunity for the rare privilege of watching another director at work as Taymor creates a new opera, Grendel.

WaPageTitle.value: Julie Taymor and Selina Cartmell - Video

pageTitle.value: Joan Jonas and Thao Nguyen Phan - Video

pageCaption.value: “Art is a spiritual practice,” says Joan Jonas, pioneer of performance and video art. For her Vietnamese protégée Thao Nguyen Phan, the experience of working with Jonas and observing her constant experimentation has taught her to be more open to what she can use to tell the stories that underlie her work.

WaPageTitle.value: Joan Jonas and Thao Nguyen Phan - Video

pageTitle.value: Peter Zumthor and Gloria Cabral - Video

pageCaption.value: Gloria Cabral, from Asunción in Paraguay, spent weeks at the Swiss studio of her mentor Peter Zumthor, who involved her in the whole process of building a tea chapel in South Korea, opening up her mind “in a radical way”.

pageCaption.value: Russian-born choreographer Alexei Ratmansky and his protégé Myles Thatcher share a deep respect for the “language” that is classical dance. Both are intensely involved in reinvigorating their art form for the 21st century. A perfect partnership.
